channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Silent Failures: The Bug That Won't Page You
item.description: A silent failure is when a service stops logging and no alert fires. Here's how absence detection catches the bug that never throws an error. The webhook consumer died at 2:41am. OOM kill — the...

item.title: Why MCP Hookups, Not Just Models, enable Agent Potential in Production
item.description: The unsexy part of the agent story is the part that decides whether it ships or stalls. Frontier models can plan, refactor, write tests, and read your codebase. What they can't do, on their own,...

item.title: JWT Validation Against an RSA Public Key: The ValidAlgorithms Gotcha
item.description: If you're validating JWTs signed with RSA in .NET and getting a signature validation failure even though you're certain the public key is correct, there's a good chance the problem isn't your key...

item.title: Karpathy ปล่อย LLM Council — เมื่อ AI หลายตัวถกเถียงกันก่อนตอบคุณ
item.description: Karpathy ปล่อย LLM Council — เมื่อ AI หลายตัวถกเถียงกันก่อนตอบคุณ โดย Nokka (นก-กา) | 5 กรกฎาคม 2026 TL;DR — สำหรับคนที่รีบ Andrej Karpathy ปล่อยโปรเจกต์ Open-Source ชื่อ LLM Council ที่ให้ LLM...

item.title: Discord accidentally banned over 8,000 people for posting grids and other ‘benign’ images
item.description: Discord says a bug affecting its safety system caused it to mistakenly ban more than 8,000 accounts since May. The platform's statement follows a wave of reports from users over the past week, who...

item.title: The Illusion of Enterprise AI: Why 95% of Current Solutions Will Fail Your Security Audit (And How DeepTech Solves It)
item.description: Over the past year, every company has suddenly wanted its own "AI Agent". The market is flooded with integrators promising "sovereign" AI deployments in a matter of days. The technical reality is...

item.title: Shipping Docs, Rust Tooling, and a Week of Deep Reviews
item.description: 14 commits, 2 PRs, 1 issue, and 5 reviews across 3 repos. This week was all about hardening my Rust CLI 'trx' with mdBook and format checks, while doing some heavy lifting on Python backend...

item.title: Running Code Review with Local AI (No Cloud, No Waiting)
item.description: Running Code Review with Local AI (No Cloud, No Waiting) Your pull request sits in queue waiting for review. It's 3 AM. Your coworker's asleep. You need feedback now. This is where most people...

item.title: We read the source of 16 sandbox SDKs before writing ours
item.description: A source-level teardown of 16 sandbox and compute SDKs, and the design decisions that came out of it: method-aware retries, typed errors with metadata, zero runtime dependencies. Before we wrote a...

item.title: Dev log #6 Networking Deep Dives and Scaling Docs: My 30k Line Week in OSS
item.description: 16 commits, 13 PRs, 4 issues, and 13 reviews across 7 repos. This week was a massive balancing act between deep p2p networking fixes in Python, scaling documentation with mdBook, and a heavy...

item.title: I ran 29 real docker-compose projects on Apple's container runtime. Here's what broke
item.description: Docker Desktop keeps a ~7.8 GB Linux VM provisioned the entire time it's running — even when zero containers are up. Apple's new container runtime idles at ~58 MB of helper processes and only...

item.title: Anker’s noise-blocking earbuds for sleeping are nearly half off
item.description: You might have a great bed and a good sleepy time routine, but if you’re still struggling to fall (or stay) asleep, Anker’s Soundcore Sleep A20 are worth considering. They’re designed to help...

item.title: Public GitHub Issue Could Trick GitHub Agentic Workflows Into Leaking Private Repo Data
item.description: A public issue can trick GitHub Agentic Workflows into leaking the contents of an organization's private repositories, researchers at Noma Security have shown. The attacker needs only to open a...

item.title: iRobot’s newest floor cleaner isn’t a robot
item.description: iRobot just announced its first-ever non-robotic floor cleaner. The $399 Roomba Electro Plus is a 5-in-1 hard-floor cleaner that combines vacuuming, mopping, and disinfecting, but you have to...

item.title: Microsoft fixes storage-hogging Windows 11 folder
item.description: Microsoft is addressing a Windows 11 bug that caused a folder to take up several gigabytes of storage space. As spotted earlier by Windows Latest, Microsoft included the patch in its optional June...

item.title: Court Filing Reveals Windows Device ID Helped FBI Trace Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker
item.description: U.S. prosecutors linked an alleged Scattered Spider hacker to a break-in at a luxury jewelry retailer using a persistent Windows device ID, according to a newly unsealed federal complaint....

item.title: Writer AI Flaw Could Let Agent Previews Leak Session Tokens Across Tenants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched critical session isolation vulnerability in Writer, an enterprise generative artificial intelligence (AI) platform, that could...

item.title: These New Smart Glasses From Solos Come With a Privacy Shield for the Cameras
item.description: You can clip a cover over the cameras, which could be a double-edged sword.

item.title: Solos debuts an even lighter version of its camera-less smart glasses
item.description: Solos announced a new version of its AirGo smart glasses, one that forgoes cameras for a sleeker design and an AI assistant that relies on voice interactions. Last year's AirGo A5 weighed 36 to 40...

item.title: Google Search lets creators know more about their reach
item.description: Google is going to give content creators and website owners a better idea of how people find their social media profiles and YouTube content through Search. With a new feature in the Google Search...

item.title: iFixit has a new toolkit for fixing appliances, building furniture, and household repairs
item.description: iFixit is best known for its detailed gadget teardowns and toolkits designed to help you crack open and troubleshoot your own electronics. Today it announced a new toolkit that's instead tailored...

item.title: The Download: your stake in OpenAI, and the Treasury’s AI warning
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI Sam Altman’s proposal...

item.title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
item.description: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.

item.title: Marshall upgrades the bass and repairability of two wireless speakers
item.description: Marshall announced new versions of its Acton and Stanmore Bluetooth speakers today with upgraded tweeters, bass ports, and internal designs that improve their ability to fill a room with sound....

item.title: Xbox’s bold plan for the future sounds nearly impossible
item.description: It's another bad week for the video game industry. Microsoft outlined a series of layoffs on Monday that Xbox CEO Asha Sharma described as "the most significant restructure in Xbox history." But...

item.title: What Changes When Your Software Supply Chain Includes AI Writing Your Code?
item.description: Software supply chain security was hard enough. Then AI joined the build pipeline. For five years, "software supply chain security" meant one question: what's in your code? Which open-source...

item.title: The foundational elements of AI architecture that IT leaders need to scale
item.description: With the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the move to agentic systems, organizations are expanding their use cases as the technology continues to grow. That constant evolution also introduces...

item.title: Are you ready for what it takes to stop ghost guns?
item.description: In the summer of 2024, former Army National Guard member Andrew Scott Hastings spent a sweaty afternoon carefully packing boxes with parts he made using his 3D printer. These weren't novelty...

item.title: Google Is Suing Chinese Scammers Who Are Using Gemini
item.description: Not sure this will have any effect, but I support the effort: According to Google’s legal filing, Outsider Enterprise operates through Telegram. The group offers phishing-as-a-service to...

item.title: Why worms (and microbes) are catching on as a manure pollution solution
item.description: Anthony Agueda, a third-generation California dairy farmer, pulls a rake through a bed of dark, wet wood chips on his family’s land in Hickman, a tiny town in the state’s agricultural heartland....
